free_text: CROSS SECTION OF O-16 + O-16 NEAR THE COULOMB BARRIER
O-16 TARGETS WERE PREPARED BY ANODIZING CLEAN 0.4 MM
THICK TA METAL SHEETS. TARGET THICKNESSES OF 290 +- 15
GAMMA-RAY SPECTRA WERE ACCUMULATED IN ENERGY REGION
FROM 7.95 TO 13.83 MEV C.M. FOR EACH VALUE OF CROSS
SECTION THE ENERGY LOSS IN TARGET WAS TAKEN INTO
THE INTENSITY OF THE 302 KEV TA-181 LINE FROM
THE COULOMB EXCITATION WAS USED AS THE STANDARD TO
OBTAIN THE PARTIAL CROSS SECTIONS. THE VALUE OF
SHEETS 43,289(84) AND THE ENERGY AND ANGULAR
DEPENDENCE ACCORDING TO K.ALDER+, COULOMB EXCITATION,
UNCERTAINTY OF REPORTED PARTIAL CROSS SECTION WAS:
15% IN N CHANNEL, 21% IN P CHANNEL,
17% IN A CHANNEL, 20% IN A+P CHANNEL,
- UNCERTAINTIES IN TARGET THICKNESS AND STOICHIOMETRY
